label: M1_DCM-SchemaRegistry
info:
Backend flows for Schema Registry Management.

════════════════════════════════════════════════
  HOW TO SET YOUR OPENAI API KEY
════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Double-click this flow tab name (DCM-SchemaRegistry)
2. In the Edit Flow panel, click the 'Environment Variables' section
3. Find the variable named: OPENAI_API_KEY
4. Replace PUT_OPENAI_API_KEY_HERE with your real OpenAI API key
   (e.g. sk-proj-abc123...)
5. Click Done, then Deploy

All OpenAI request nodes in this flow use this single key.
Do NOT paste the key anywhere else.

════════════════════════════════════════════════

OpenAI calls use real http request nodes — not simulated.

MongoDB collections used:
  sr_prompts        – prompt records
  sr_test_cases     – saved test cases
  sr_llm_configs    – LLM configuration records
  sr_providers      – multi-model provider records
  sr_local_schemas  – local schema definitions
  sr_mappings       – schema mapping configurations

All responses are sent back to the Dashboard via link-out → dashboard-linkin.

label: M2_DCM-CatalogueRegistry
info:
Backend flows for Catalogue Registry.

MongoDB collection: cr_catalogues

Operations:
  - registerRemoteCatalog (insertOne)
  - getCatalogRegistry (find all)
  - updateRemoteCatalog (updateOne with patch)
  - deleteRemoteCatalog (deleteOne)
  - uploadCatalogJson (bulk insertMany)

Asset Types (FR-CR-02):
  - listAssetTypes (find)
  - saveAssetType (insertOne)
  - updateAssetType (updateOne with patch)
  - deleteAssetType (deleteOne)
MongoDB collection: cr_asset_types

All responses are sent back to the Dashboard via link-out → dashboard-linkin (1320340df737c22e).

label: M3_DCM-Harvester
info:
Backend flows for Asset Crawler + Mapper (Harvester).

MongoDB collections:
  hv_runs         – harvest run records (config, status, results, embedded logs + provenance)
  lc_assets       – local catalogue assets (written during harvest, owned by M5_DCM-LocalCatalogue)
  cr_catalogues   – catalogue registry (read-only, shared)

Operations:
  - getHarvestCatalogues (reads from cr_catalogues)
  - startHarvest (creates run, generates assets into lc_assets, stores log + provenance)
  - listHarvestRuns (find all runs)
  - getHarvestRunDetail (find one run)
  - listHarvestLogs (extract logs from runs)
  - listHarvestProvenance (extract provenance from runs)
  - pauseHarvest / resumeHarvest / cancelHarvest (update run status)

SRS Requirements covered:
  FR-ACM-01: Initiating a Harvest
  FR-ACM-02: Harvest Scope
  FR-ACM-03: Lifecycle of Imported Assets
  FR-ACM-04: Linked Assets
  FR-ACM-05: Crawling References

All responses are sent back to the Dashboard via link-out → dashboard-linkin.

label: M4_DCM-AdminTools
info:
Backend flows for Access Control & Admin Tools.

MongoDB collections:
  - dcm_users
  - dcm_roles
  - dcm_audit_log
  - auth_users
  - auth_sessions

Operations:
  - listUsers, getUserDetail, createUser, updateUser, deleteUser
  - listRoles, createRole, updateRole, deleteRole
  - listAudit
  - getAdminTools (legacy compat)

All operations go through the permission gate.

SRS Requirements covered:
  FR-AC-01: User Roles - CRUD for all areas

label: M5_DCM-LocalCatalogue
info:
Backend flow for Local Catalogue.

MongoDB collection:
  lc_assets – normalized local catalogue asset records
             populated by Harvest (M3), managed here.

Operations:
  getLocalCatalogue  – list all lc_assets
  getLocalAssetDetail – get single asset detail
  deleteLocalAsset   – delete by uniqueId
  updateLocalAsset   – update fields by uniqueId
  getLocalProvenance – list provenance (extracted from hv_runs)
  getLocalStats      – summary stats

All responses are sent back to the Dashboard via link-out → dashboard-linkin (c48570326bcd6b3d).

label: M6_DCM-Auth
info:
Backend flows for Authentication, Invitation, and Session Management.

MongoDB collections:
  - auth_users
  - auth_sessions
  - dcm_users
  - dcm_roles
  - dcm_audit_log

Operations:
  - login: validate credentials (bcrypt + legacy plaintext fallback)
  - checkAuth: verify session token
  - logOut: invalidate session
  - hydrateSession: return user with roles + flattened permissions
  - changePassword: update password (gated)

Default admin seeded on deploy:
  - username: admin / password: 1234
  - Also seeds dcm_users row and 3 system roles (admin, editor, viewer)

name: set msg.db
set msg.db.error
write upload data
set http response
command unzip
func:
const name = msg.uibuildername;
msg.command =
    `sh -c 'ZIP="/data/uibuilder/backend-dist.zip"; ` +
    `DST="/data/uibuilder/${name}/src"; ` +
    `TMP="$(mktemp -d)"; ` +
    `mkdir -p "$DST"; ` +
    `unzip -q -o "$ZIP" -d "$TMP"; ` +
    `SRC="$TMP/dist"; [ -d "$SRC" ] || SRC="$TMP"; ` +
    `rm -rf "$DST"/*; ` +
    `cp -a "$SRC"/. "$DST"/; ` +
    `chmod -R u+rw,g+rw,o+rw "$DST"; ` +
    `rm -rf "$TMP"'`;

return msg;

══ SETUP: Double-click the flow tab → set OPENAI_API_KEY env var ══
info:
WHERE TO PUT YOUR OPENAI API KEY:

1. Double-click the tab name 'DCM-SchemaRegistry' at the top
2. Open 'Environment Variables'
3. Replace PUT_OPENAI_API_KEY_HERE with your real key
4. Click Done → Deploy

All OpenAI-dependent operations (enhance prompt, code generation)
use real http request nodes that POST to
https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions.

Function nodes are used ONLY for:
  – payload shaping / request preparation
  – response parsing
  – validation and status updates
  – MongoDB query preparation
  – dry-run code execution

saveProvider: encrypt apiKey + KMS check (AES-256-GCM)
info:
Encrypts LLM provider API keys using AES-256-GCM.
Reads encryption key from process.env.DCM_KMS_KEY.
Provides encryptSecret and decryptSecret helpers.
Algorithm: aes-256-gcm

DOC: encrypt — uses AES-256-GCM via crypto.createCipheriv('aes-256-gcm', ...)
info: function encryptSecret(plaintext) — see saveProvider node ee02000000000010

name: DOC: decrypt — uses AES-256-GCM via crypto.createDecipheriv('aes-256-gcm', ...)
info: function decryptSecret(obj) — see saveProvider node ee02000000000010

name: DOC: KMS — env var process.env.DCM_KMS_KEY (32-byte hex key for AES)
info: DCM_KMS_KEY must be set in the environment before any provider write handler executes.

name: PERM: createPrompt requires schema.registry.create — else permission_denied
PERM: updatePrompt / updatePromptStatus require schema.registry.update — else permission_denied
PERM: deletePrompt / deleteProvider / deleteRemoteSchema require schema.registry.delete — else permission_denied
PERM: listPrompts / listProviders / listRemoteSchemas / listLocalSchemas require schema.registry.read — else permission_denied
PERM: saveProvider / saveLocalSchema / saveRemoteSchema require schema.registry.create — else permission_denied
PERM: setSystemSetting / activateLocalSchemaVersion require schema.registry.update — else permission_denied
PERM: getLlmUsage / getSystemSettings / listPromptVersions require schema.registry.read — else permission_denied
PERM: deleteLocalSchema / deleteMapping require schema.registry.delete — else permission_denied
PERM: validateSampleAsset / saveMapping require schema.registry.update — else permission_denied

══ Authentication Backend ══
info:
Handles user authentication, session management, and credential validation.

Responses go back to Dashboard uibuilder via link-out → dashboard-linkin.

name: ── LOGIN ──
info:
1. Receives login request with username/password
2. Looks up user in auth_users collection
3. Verifies password (bcrypt compare with plaintext fallback for seeded admin)
4. Returns success with session token, user info, roles, and flattened permissions

name: prepare login lookup
verify password & build session
resolve roles → permissions
flatten permissions & build login response
restore login response
func:
msg.payload = msg._savedResponse;
return msg;

name: ── CHECK AUTH ──
info: Validates an existing session token against auth_sessions collection.

name: prepare session lookup
validate session
── LOGOUT ──
info: Removes session from database.

name: prepare session delete
logout response
── HYDRATE SESSION ──
info:
Returns user with roles and flattened permissions for the current session.
Called after login to populate the frontend store.

name: hydrate: find session
hydrate: lookup dcm_user
hydrate: find roles
hydrate: build response
── VERIFY INVITATION (pre-auth) ──
info: Validates an invitation token. No auth required.

name: changePassword (stub)
── SEED DEFAULT USER + SYSTEM ROLES ──
info:
On deploy, ensures:
1. Default admin in auth_users
2. Default admin in dcm_users
3. System roles (admin, editor, viewer) in dcm_roles
